data.narrative:
Milrem Robotics is a Series B–stage defense and security company that has raised $30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of 10 companies spanning the Defense & Security sector: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies at equivalent or proximate stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $30M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $57M. The peer-median method references 6 same-sector, same-stage private companies—Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, Defense Unicorns, and one additional peer—with valuations ranging $1.0B to $1.5B and a median of $1.2B. This substantial divergence reflects material differences in revenue traction, customer concentration, or technology maturity between Milrem and high-valuation defense-tech peers.

The triangulated analysis supports a range from $48M (low, based on stage-progression) to $1.4B (high, anchored to peer outliers), with a median of $628M. The wide dispersion underscores significant uncertainty regarding Milrem's position within the defense robotics market relative to better-capitalized competitors. Resolution requires clarity on: current revenue run-rate and customer diversification, proprietary technology defensibility, and path to profitability relative to peer benchmarks.
